title: SSC CGL 2026 Sectional Timing Tier I 43 43

questions.question: Select the option in which the following figure is embedded.(Rotation is not allowed) ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p26758/image028.png) ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p26758/image029.jpg)
questions.explanation: Here, the given figure is embedded in figure (IV). ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p26758/image030.png) Thus, the answer is option (d).

questions.question: January 12, 1965 is Tuesday. May 30, 1965 will be?
questions.explanation: It is given that, January 12, 1965 is Tuesday. May 30, 1965 is the 138th day after January 12, 1965. [ 138 - 19 days in January + 28 days in February + 31 days in March + 30 days in April + 30 days in May ] Each day of the week is repeated after 7 days. So on 138th day (19 weeks and 5 days) it will be a Sunday.

questions.question: In the following question, select the odd one from the given alternatives.
questions.explanation: K (L M) N (OP) Q C (DE) F (GH) I T (UV) W (XY) Z N (O) P (Q) R.

questions.question: A situation is given, followed by two conclusions I and II. Read the situation and both the conclusions carefully and select the correct answer. Situation: Eating overcooked food is generally not considered healthy. Overcooking can cause the breakdown of vitamins, minerals, and other beneficial compounds in food, diminishing their nutritional values. Conclusions: I. Essential minerals volatilize as a result of overcooking. II. Food is more easily metabolized when it is overcooked.
options: Both I and II can be concluded
Neither I nor II can be concluded
questions.explanation: The situation states that overcooking is bad for health because it depletes a food's nutritional value and causes the key minerals and vitamins to break down. Conclusion I: Due to overcooking, vital vitamins and minerals disappear as a result of overcooking. So, conclusion I is true. Conclusion II: According to the situation, it is harder to metabolize food when it is overcooked. So, conclusion II is false.

questions.question: Read the situation and choose the best judgment: At a metro station, the gate opens without deducting fare due to a system glitch. You already entered the paid area. A staffed counter is visible near the platform. Question: Given this situation, which of the following is the most ethical and responsible action?
options: Proceed since it is a system fault and not your responsibility.
Approach the counter, report the non-deduction, and pay the correct fare with a manual receipt.
Exit and re-enter hoping the gate deducts next time.
Board and later post feedback in the app without paying.
questions.explanation: Paying the accurate fare and creating a record resolves the discrepancy and protects you from penalties. Relying on the fault or deferring action exploits the system and risks fines later.

questions.question: Which of the following numbers will replace the question mark (?) in the given series? 3, 4, 8, 17, 33, ?
questions.explanation: The difference between the numbers is 1, 4, 9, 16, 25 (consecutive square values). hence the missing number: 33+25 = 58

questions.question: Which two signs should be interchanged to make the below equation mathematically correct? 56 x 7 - 25 + 792 ÷ 8 = 318
questions.explanation: Option (a): 56 x 7 - 25 + 792 ÷ 8 = 318 After interchanging: 56 x 7 + 25 - 792 ÷ 8 = 318 392 + 25 - 99 = 318 417 - 99 = 318 So, 318 is equal to 318 Option (b): 56 x 7 - 25 + 792 ÷ 8 = 318 After interchanging: 56 - 7 x 25 + 792 ÷ 8 = 318 56 - 175 + 99 = 318 - 20 is not equal to 318 Option (c): 56 x 7 - 25 + 792 ÷ 8 = 318 After interchanging: 56 + 7 - 25 x 792 ÷ 8 = 318 63 - 25 x 99 = 318 63 - 2475 = 318 - 2412 is not equal to 318 Option (d): 56 x 7 - 25 + 792 ÷ 8 = 318 After interchanging: 56 x 7 - 25 ÷ 792 + 8 = 318 392 - 0.031 + 8 = 318 399.96 is not equal to 318

questions.question: Vinu runs for 2 km to the west and he takes a right and runs for 11 km. He again takes a right turn and runs for 9 km and finally he walks for 5 km after turning towards the south. What is the approximate shortest distance between the initial and final points?
questions.explanation: Here, the approximate shortest distance = √[ (11 - 5)2 + (9 - 2)2 ] = √[ (6)2 + (7)2 ] = √[ (36) + (49) ] = √[ 85 ] = 9.21 = 9 km approximately ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p13650/image017.png)

questions.question: Read the situation and choose the best judgment: During an internal review, you find a revenue misclassification that overstates quarterly earnings by a small percentage. The CFO suggests "fixing it next quarter."
options: Agree because the percentage is immaterial to most readers.
Note it privately for your files and move on.
Escalate via the documented audit/escalation protocol to the audit committee or independent reviewers and record the issue formally.
Resign immediately without reporting to avoid complicity.
questions.explanation: Formal escalation ensures accurate reporting and independent oversight. "Immaterial" judgments require governance, not unilateral acceptance. Silent notes neither correct the report nor protect stakeholders. Resignation without reporting leaves the misstatement unaddressed.

questions.question: In a zoo, the number of birds is 2.4 times the number of animals. If eight birds are taken out of the zoo then the number of birds is 2 times the number of animals. What will be the number of animals if seven more animals are added to the zoo ?
questions.explanation: It is given that, The number of birds is 2.4 times the number of animals. B = 2.4 A ........(1) If eight birds are taken out of the zoo then the number of birds is 2 times the number of animals. B - 8 = 2 x A B = 2A + 8........(2) From (1) and (2), 2.4 A = 2A + 8 0.4 A = 8 A = 20 The number of animals if seven more animals are added to the zoo = 20 + 7 = 27.

questions.question: Select the option which is related to the fifth term in the same way as the second term is related to the first term and the fourth term is related to the third term. RA : 19 : : XE : 29 : : UG : ?
questions.explanation: Here, the place value of the alphabets in English alphabetical order are considered. 12345678910111213 ABCDEFGHIJKLM 14151617181920212223242526 NOPQRSTUVWXYZ RA -- 18 + 1 = 19; XE -- 24 + 5 = 29 Similarly, UG -- 21 + 7 = 28

questions.question: 'M >> N' means 'N is the father of M'. 'M {} N' means 'N is the paternal grandfather of M'. 'M () N' means 'N is the sister of M.' 'M ?? N' means 'N is the son of M'. If A ?? C >> T ?? Y {} U () X, then how is A related to X?
questions.explanation: It is given that: 'M >> N' means 'N is the father of M'. 'M {} N' means 'N is the paternal grandfather of M'. 'M () N' means 'N is the sister of M.' 'M ?? N' means 'N is the son of M'. After decoding the expression, A ?? C >> T ?? Y {} U () X, we get: C is the son of A. T is the father of C. Y is the son of T. U is the paternal grandfather of Y. X is the sister of U. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p22277/image026.jpg) So, A is the daughter-in-law of X's brother.

questions.question: K is the sister of P, who is the husband of M. G is the sister-in-law of M. A is the son of M. R is the father-in-law of M. S is the mother of K. How is G related to A?
questions.explanation: G is the aunt of A. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p2113/image027.jpg)

questions.question: Choose a word which would resemble the mirror image of the given word. R A O b V x 0 Y T i * B (I) ꓭ * i T Y 0 Z x V p O A Я (II)ꓭ * T Y 0 x V d b O A Я (III)ꓭ * i T Y 0 x V d A v R (IV) ꓭ * i T Y 0 x V d O A Я
questions.explanation: The word IV would resemble the mirror image of the given word.

questions.question: If 'K' is replaced by '-', 'L' is replaced by '÷', 'M' is replaced by '+' and 'N' is replaced by 'x', then find the value of the following equation. 52N3M18K66L2
questions.explanation: We have: 52N3M18K66L2 After replacing letters with sign, we get: 52 x 3 +18 - 66 ÷ 2 = 156 + 18 - 33 = 141

questions.question: Select the option which can replace the question mark (?) in the given series. 342, 413, 486, 565, 648, ?, 834
questions.explanation: Logic for the above series - 342 + 71 = 413 413 + 73 = 486 486 + 79 = 565 565 + 83 = 648 648 + 89 = 737 737 + 97 = 834 (The numbers added are consecutive prime numbers.)

questions.question: Each of the digits in the number 6915374 is arranged in descending order from left to right. The position of how many digits will remain unchanged as compared to that in the original number?
questions.explanation: 6915374 After arranging above number in descending order from left to right - 6915374 9765431 Only one digit i.e. '5' will remain unchanged as compared to that in the original number.

questions.question: Fill in the missing value in the series: L27O,__, H125S, F36U, D343W
questions.explanation: Here, the pattern is: For the 1st alphabet of each term: L-2= J; J-2= H; H-2= F; F-2= D. For the 2nd alphabet of each term: O+2= Q; Q+2= S; S+2= U; U+2= W. For numbers: 33= 27, 42= 16, 53=125, 62=36, 73= 343.

questions.question: In a certain code language, 'ANGEL' is coded as '261320415' and 'HUMID' is coded as '192014823'. How will 'BLINK' be coded in that language?
questions.explanation: Here, the pattern is: 'ANGEL' is coded as '261320415'. A = The reverse alphabet of A according to the English alphabetical series is Z and Z = 26. N - 1 = 14 - 1 = 13. G = The reverse alphabet of G according to the English alphabetical series is T and T = 20. E - 1 = 5 - 1 = 4 L = The reverse alphabet of L according to the English alphabetical series is O and O = 15. Similarly, 'HUMID' is coded as '192014823'. H = The reverse alphabet of H according to the English alphabetical series is S and S = 19. U - 1 = 22 - 1 = 21 M = The reverse alphabet of M according to the English alphabetical series is N and N = 14. I - 1 = 9 - 1 = 8 D = The reverse alphabet of D according to the English alphabetical series is W and W = 23. Therefore, B = The reverse alphabet of B according to the English alphabetical series is Y and Y = 25. L - 1 = 12 - 1 = 11. I = The reverse alphabet of I according to the English alphabetical series is R and R = 18. N - 1 = 14 - 1 = 13 K = The reverse alphabet of K according to the English alphabetical series is P and P = 16.

questions.question: In a certain coded language, 'ADJUDICATORY' is coded as 'IDUJDAYROTAC', and 'GASTROPLASTY' is coded as 'ORTSAGYTSALP'. How will 'QUADRICOPTER' be coded in that language?
questions.explanation: 'ADJUDICATORY' is coded as 'IDUJDAYROTAC' First, the half part of the word is reversed = IDUJDA Then, after that second half part of the word is reversed = IDUJDAYROTAC 'GASTROPLASTY' is coded as '' First, the half part of the word is reversed = ORTSAG Then, after that second half part of the word is reversed = ORTSAGYTSALP Similarly, 'QUADRICOPTER' First, the half part of the word is reversed = IRDAUQ Then, after that second half part of the word is reversed = IRDAUQRETPOC 'QUADRICOPTER' will be coded as 'IRDAUQRETPOC'.

questions.question: Select the option that is related to the fifth letter-cluster in the same way as the second letter-cluster is related to the first letter-cluster and the fourth letter-cluster is related to the third letter-cluster. PFG : QCJ :: BMC : CJF :: KPS : ?
questions.explanation: We have: ABCDEFGHIJKLM 12345678910111213 NOPQRSTUVWXYZ 14151617181920212223242526 P + 1 = Q F - 3 = C G + 3 = J B + 1 = C M - 3 = J C + 3 = F K + 1 = L P - 3 = M S + 3 = V

questions.question: Read the given statements and conclusions carefully. Assuming that the information given in the statements is true, even if it appears to be at variance with commonly known facts, decide which of the given conclusions logically follow(s) from the statements. Statements: All stone is rock All sand is stone No rock is soil Conclusions: I. No sand is soil II. No soil is stone III. All rock is sand
options: Only conclusions I and II follow
Only conclusions II and III follow
questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p20772/image002.jpg) No sand is soil: Here, all sand is rock and no rock is soil. Thus, no sand is soil. So, conclusion I is true. No soil is stone: here, all stone is rock and no rock is soil. So, no stone is soil. So, conclusion II is true. All rock is sand: Here, there is a possibility that all rock is sand. So, conclusion III is false.

questions.question: Study the given pattern carefully and select the number that can replace the question mark (?) in it. 12 ? 14 13 18 9 50 76 46
If 21 ψ 15 Σ 9 = 35 and 64 ψ 12 Σ 32 = 24, then 28 ψ 13 Σ 7 = ?
questions.explanation: 21 ψ 15 Σ 9 = 35 and 64 ψ 12 Σ 32 = 24 Here, the pattern is: 21 x 15 ÷ 9 = 35 64 x 12 ÷ 32 = 24 Similarly, 28 x 13 ÷ 7 = 52

questions.question: How many meaningful four-letter English word(s) starting with 'D' can be formed using the second, sixth and seventh letters of the word CONSUME (when counted from left to right) using each letter only once in each word?
questions.explanation: The given word is CONSUME. The second, sixth and seventh letters of the word from left to right are O, M and E. The word should start with D. So, D _ _ _ Thus, the meaningful words are DOME, DEMO.

questions.question: Match the following fiscal terms with their descriptions: Column A (Fiscal Term) Column B (Description) A. Fiscal Deficit 1. Excess of revenue expenditure over revenue receipts B. Revenue Deficit 2. Borrowings + Other Liabilities C. Primary Deficit 3. Fiscal deficit - Interest payments
questions.explanation: A. Fiscal Deficit is the difference between the government's total expenditure and its total receipts excluding borrowings. It represents the total borrowing requirement of the government, hence it matches with Borrowings + Other Liabilities. B. Revenue Deficit is the shortfall of revenue receipts compared to revenue expenditure. It matches with Excess of revenue expenditure over revenue receipts. C. Primary Deficit is the fiscal deficit of the current year minus the interest payments on previous borrowings. It matches with Fiscal deficit - Interest payments. The correct combination is A-2, B-1, C-3.

questions.question: The Rig Veda contains ______ hymns.
questions.explanation: The Rig Veda consists of 1028 hymns, organised into ten books known as mandalas. Each mandala has sūktas (hymns) formed by individual strophes called ric from which the name Rig Veda. The philological and linguistic evidence indicates that the Rig Veda is one of the oldest existing texts in any Indo-European language and that probably originated from the region of present day Pakistan, between 1500 and 1200 BCE.

questions.question: On 1 January 2024, ISRO launched the XPoSat mission. What is its primary objective?
options: To study high-energy cosmic X-rays from celestial sources such as black holes and neutron stars
To monitor Earth's vegetation and climatic changes
To test human spaceflight systems in low Earth orbit
questions.explanation: The primary objective of the XPoSat mission is to study the polarization of X-rays emanating from intense celestial sources like black holes, neutron stars, and active galactic nuclei. Explanation of Options: (a) To study high-energy cosmic X-rays from celestial sources such as black holes and neutron stars: This is the correct answer. XPoSat (X-ray Polarimeter Satellite) is India's first dedicated mission to measure the polarization of X-rays. Polarization provides a unique insight into the geometry of emission regions, magnetic field structures, and the physical processes occurring in and around extreme cosmic objects. (b) To monitor Earth's vegetation and climatic changes: This describes the objective of Earth Observation satellites, such as those in the ISRO's EOS (formerly Resourcesat/Cartosat) series. These satellites use optical, infrared, or radar instruments to study the Earth's surface, not cosmic X-rays. (c) To test human spaceflight systems in low Earth orbit: This is the objective of missions related to India's Gaganyaan program. These involve test flights to validate systems like the crew escape system, crew module, and life support, not astronomical research. (d) To study the Sun's chromosphere: This is the primary objective of a solar observatory. ISRO's dedicated mission for this purpose is Aditya-L1, which was launched in 2023 to study the Sun's outer layers from the L1 Lagrange point. Further insights: Significance: XPoSat is a groundbreaking mission for India, making it only the second country in the world (after the United States with its IXPE mission) to have a dedicated observatory for X-ray polarimetry. Payloads: The satellite carries two main scientific payloads: POLIX (Polarimeter Instrument in X-rays): This is the primary instrument, designed to measure the degree and angle of polarization of X-rays in the medium energy range. XSPECT (X-ray Spectroscopy and Timing): This instrument will provide simultaneous spectroscopic and timing data, giving context to the polarimetry measurements. Scientific Goals: By studying the polarization of X-rays, scientists hope to better understand the behavior of matter in extreme gravitational and magnetic fields, such as those found near black holes and on the surface of neutron stars.

questions.question: Which of the statements given below is/are not correct? 1) PM SVANidhi gives working-capital micro loans to street vendors without collateral. 2) Atal Pension Yojana is open to citizens aged 18 - 40 and assures a minimum pension from age 60. 3) Kisan Credit Card is meant only for salaried employees of public sector banks.
questions.explanation: Statements 1 and 2 are correct. Statement 3 is incorrect; KCC targets farmers (including allied activities), not salaried employees. Further Insights: - PM SVANidhi initial loan is typically up to ₹10,000 with incentives for digital transactions and timely repayment. - APY offers guaranteed pension slabs (e.g., ₹1,000 - ₹5,000 per month) with government co-contribution earlier for eligible subscribers. - KCC has been extended to animal husbandry and fisheries; provides flexible credit with interest subvention in some cases.

questions.question: The first Paralympic Games were held in ______.
questions.explanation: The first ever Paralympic Games were held in Rome, Italy, in 1960. Taking place soon after the conclusion of the Olympic Games, the Olympic-style games for disabled athletes attracted 400 participants from 23 countries, who competed in 8 sports. Taking place every four years in the same year as the Olympic Games, the Paralympic Games have since grown into one of the world's biggest sport events, with a track record for driving social inclusion.

questions.question: Pandit Shivnath Mishra founded the first sitar gharana of ________
questions.explanation: Pandit Shivnath Mishra founded the first sitar gharana of Banaras. He captured the essence of the gayaki - the singing style - of the Banaras gharana on his sitar, a style of playing often called the "first sitar gharana" of Banaras. Academy of Indian Classical Music is an institution he founded in 1999.

questions.question: Nagorno-Karabakh region was recently mentioned in the news. It is a disputed region between which of the following countries?
questions.explanation: - Nagorno-Karabakh region is a disputed region between Armenia and Azerbaijan. - Nagorno-Karabakh is a mountainous and heavily forested region that under international law is recognised as part of Azerbaijan. - However, ethnic Armenians who constitute the vast majority of the population there reject Azeri rule (the legal system of Azerbaijan). - After Azerbaijan's troops were pushed out of the region following a war in the 1990s, these ethnic Armenians have been in administrative control of Nagorno-Karabakh, with support from Armenia.

questions.question: Identify the correct statement(s) regarding ISRO Missions. 1) Aditya-L1 studies the Sun from a halo orbit around the Sun - Earth L1 point. 2) NISAR (NASA - ISRO) is planned for a Sun - Earth L2 orbit. 3) The Chandrayaan-2 orbiter continues to operate after the lander's crash.
questions.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: Aditya-L1 operates around L1 for continuous solar observations. Statement 2 is incorrect: NISAR is a low Earth orbit (near-polar/Sun-synchronous) mission. Statement 3 is correct: The Chandrayaan-2 orbiter remains operational. Further Insights: - Chandrayaan-3 achieved a soft landing near the lunar south polar region in 2023. - LVM3 (GSLV Mk III) is ISRO's heaviest operational launcher. - NavIC (IRNSS) provides regional navigation over India and ~1,500 km beyond. - Gaganyaan targets human spaceflight in LEO with uncrewed test flights preceding crewed missions.

questions.question: Which of the following is a Hindu festival that marks the transition of the sun from the zodiac sign of Dhanu (Sagittarius) into the zodiac sign of Makara (Capricorn) ?
questions.explanation: Makar Sankranti is a Hindu festival that marks the transition of the sun from the zodiac sign of Dhanu (Sagittarius) into the zodiac sign of Makara (Capricorn) and marks the beginning of the sun's journey northwards, known as Uttarayan. Makar Sankranti is a significant festival for farmers as it marks the end of the harvest season and the beginning of a new agricultural cycle. It is celebrated with great enthusiasm by farmers, who thank the gods for a bountiful harvest and pray for a good crop in the coming year. Further insights: Baisakhi: This is a harvest festival primarily celebrated in Punjab and other northern regions. It marks the Sikh New Year and is associated with the spring harvest. Lohri: Another Punjabi festival, Lohri is celebrated the night before Makar Sankranti. It is associated with bonfires, winter solstice, and the harvesting of winter crops. Basant Panchmi: This festival marks the arrival of spring and is dedicated to Goddess Saraswati. It is associated with wearing yellow clothes and celebrating the spirit of knowledge and learning.

questions.question: Identify which of the following statements are correct. I.The Gas Authority of India Limited was set up in 1992 as a public sector undertaking to transport and market natural gas. II. The Atomic Energy Commission was established in 1948. III. Mumbai High was discovered in 1973 and production commenced in 1976.
questions.explanation: The Gas Authority of India Limited was set up in 1984 as a public sector undertaking to transport and market natural gas.Hence statement I is incorrect. The Atomic Energy Commission was established in 1948, progress could be made only after the establishment of the Atomic Energy Institute at Trombay in 1954 which was renamed as the Bhabha Atomic Research Centre in 1967. Mumbai High which lies 160 km off Mumbai was discovered in 1973 and production commenced in 1976.

questions.question: The Ramnath Goenka Excellence in Journalism Award is presented for achievements in ________.
questions.explanation: · Instituted by The Indian Express Group, the Ramnath Goenka Awards honour outstanding work in investigative reporting and journalism. · Categories include print, broadcast and digital media, emphasising ethics and accountability. · Recipients are recognised for news reporting, commentary, photojournalism and editorial writing. · Literature is honoured by Sahitya Akademi and Jnanpith Awards, not by Ramnath Goenka. · Film achievements are celebrated by National Film Awards and Filmfare Awards. · Science excellence is recognised by awards like the Bhatnagar Prize and Infosys Prize.

questions.question: Which of the following statements correctly describes respiration in plants?
options: Plants respire only during the night when photosynthesis stops
Respiration in plants involves the oxidation of glucose to release energy
Oxygen is released as the main product of plant respiration
Respiration in plants occurs only in green parts of the plant
questions.explanation: Respiration in plants is a continuous process in which glucose is oxidised to release energy (ATP) for growth, repair, and metabolic activities. It occurs day and night, in all living cells, and primarily consumes oxygen and releases carbon dioxide.

questions.question: Consider the following statements about the SabhaSaar tool: Statement I: SabhaSaar is an AI-powered tool launched by the Ministry of Panchayati Raj to automatically generate structured Minutes of Meeting for Panchayats. Statement II: Through its integration with the Bhashini mission, the tool currently supports all 22 scheduled languages of India. Which of the above statement is/are correct?
options: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
Neither Statement I nor Statement II is correct
questions.explanation: The Ministry of Panchayati Raj (MoPR) launched SabhaSaar, an Artificial Intelligence (AI)-powered meeting summarization tool designed to automatically generate structured Minutes of Meeting (MoM) from Gram Sabha or other Panchayat meetings' audio and video recordings. SabhaSaar uses advanced AI and Natural Language Processing (NLP) technologies to transcribe spoken discussions, identify key decisions and action points, and produce well-formatted meeting minutes. Integrated with Bhashini, the Government of India's National Language Translation Mission, the tool currently supports 13 Indian languages, ensuring inclusivity and accessibility for Panchayat functionaries across linguistic backgrounds. The number of languages supported will also gradually be increased in future.

questions.question: Match the ICAR institutions in Column I with their respective headquarters in Column II. Column I (ICAR Institutions) Column II (Headquarters) (A) ICAR-Indian Agricultural Research Institute (I) Karnal (B) ICAR-National Dairy Research Institute (II) Kochi (C) ICAR-Central Potato Research Institute (III) New Delhi (D) ICAR-Central Marine Fisheries Research Institute (IV) Shimla
questions.explanation: ICAR-Indian Agricultural Research Institute (IARI), popularly known as the Pusa Institute, is the flagship institution of ICAR and is located in New Delhi. ICAR-National Dairy Research Institute (NDRI), a premier institute for dairy research and education, is located in Karnal, Haryana. ICAR-Central Potato Research Institute (CPRI), which focuses on research for one of the world's most important food crops, is located in Shimla, Himachal Pradesh. ICAR-Central Marine Fisheries Research Institute (CMFRI), dedicated to marine fisheries research, has its headquarters in Kochi, Kerala.

questions.question: With reference to the Census 2011, consider: I. The level of urbanization in India crossed the 50% mark. II. The district of Serchhip (Mizoram) had the highest literacy rate among all districts. III. The state with the lowest literacy rate was Bihar. Which of the above statement is/are true?
questions.explanation: Statements II and III are correct, while Statement I is incorrect. Statement I is incorrect: According to the 2011 Census, the urban population in India was 377 million, which constituted 31.16% of the total population. This is well below the 50% mark. Statement II is correct: Serchhip district in Mizoram recorded the highest literacy rate of 97.91% among all districts in India as per the 2011 Census. Statement III is correct: Bihar had the lowest literacy rate among all states in India, at 61.80%.

questions.question: With reference to the Indian National Congress, consider the following statements: I. The first session of the INC was held at Bombay in 1885 under W.C. Bonnerjee's presidency. II. The Surat split of 1907 divided the INC into Moderates and Extremists. III. The Lucknow Pact of 1916 was between the Congress and the British Government. Which of the above statement are correct?
questions.explanation: Statements I and II are correct. Statement I is correct: The first session was held at Gokuldas Tejpal Sanskrit College in Bombay in December 1885, with Womesh Chunder Bonnerjee as the first president. Statement II is correct: During the Surat session in 1907, ideological differences between the Moderates and Extremists came to a head, leading to a split in the Congress. Statement III is incorrect: The Lucknow Pact of 1916 was an agreement between the Indian National Congress and the All-India Muslim League.

questions.question: Given below are two statements marked as Assertion and Reason. Choose the correct option. Assertion (A): The red color of many peninsular soils is due to ferric oxide coatings on soil particles. Reason (R): These soils commonly develop on ancient crystalline rocks under well-drained, warm conditions that favor oxidation of iron.
options: Both A and R are true, and R is the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true, but R is not the correct explanation of A
A is true, but R is false
A is false, but R is true
questions.explanation: A is true: Hematite and other ferric oxides impart the red hue. R is true and explains A: Warm, well-drained conditions promote oxidation; parent material is often granites/gneisses. Further Insights: - Major tracts: Tamil Nadu plateau, Karnataka, parts of Telangana - Andhra, Chota Nagpur, Odisha uplands. - Typically low in humus, nitrogen, and phosphorus; response to fertilizers is good. - Crops include millets, groundnut, pulses, cotton; irrigation improves yields. - Lateritization can further leach bases, producing lateritic caps on summits.

questions.question: Consider the following statements: I. M.T. Vasudevan Nair's debut novel, Randamoozham, won him the Kerala Sahitya Akademi Award. II. He was a recipient of both the Padma Bhushan and the Padma Vibhushan awards from the Government of India. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Madath Thekkepaattu Vasudevan Narayanan Nair (MT Vasudevan Nair) was an Indian author, lecturer, screenplay writer and film director. He was a prolific and versatile writer in modern Malayalam literature, and was one of the masters of post-Independence Indian literature. His first major novel, Naalukettu (The Legacy), written at the age of 23, won the Kerala Sahitya Akademi Award in 1958. His other novels include Manju (Mist), Kaalam (Time), Asuravithu (The Demon Seed), and Randamoozham (The Second Turn). Randamoozham, which retells the story of the Mahabharata from the point of view of Bhimasena, is widely credited as his masterpiece. In 2005, India's third-highest civilian honour, Padma Bhushan, was awarded to him. He died in Kozhikode on 25 December 2024. In 2025, he received Padma Vibhushan, India's second-highest civilian honour, posthumously.

questions.question: At the 87th Senior National Badminton Championships held in Vijayawada in December 2025, who clinched the Women's Singles title?
questions.explanation: Surya Charishma Tamiri clinched the Women's Singles title at the 87th Senior National Badminton Championships in Vijayawada with her victory over Tanvi Patri today. After losing the opening game, Tamiri staged a strong comeback to win the final 17 -21, 21-12, 21-14. In the Men's Singles, Rithvik Sanjeevi S defeated Bharat Raghav in straight games, 21-16, 22-20, to win the title. In Men's doubles, the pair of A. Hariharan and Ruban Kumar emerged champions after defeating Mithilesh Krishnan and Prejan 24-22, 21-17 in the final. In the women's doubles final, former champions Shikha Gautam and Ashwini Bhat K overcame Priya Devi Konjengban and Shruti Mishra 21-14, 21-18 to reclaim the title. Meanwhile, in the mixed doubles final, Sathwik Reddy K and Radhika Sharma secured the championship with a convincing 21-9, 21-15 win over Ashith Surya and Amrutha P.

questions.question: The Lanthanides consist of the elements in the f-block of period ______
questions.explanation: The Lanthanides consist of the elements in the f-block of period six in the periodic table. While these metals can be considered transition metals, they have properties that set them apart from the rest of the elements. The Lanthanides were first discovered in 1787 when a unusual black mineral was found in Ytterby, Sweden. These elements were first classified as 'rare earth' due to the fact that obtained by reasonably rare minerals. However, this is can be misleading since the Lanthanide elements have a practically unlimited abundance. The term Lanthanides was adopted, originating from the first element of the series, Lanthanum.

questions.question: Which of the following best describes Net National Product (NNP) at factor cost?
options: Gross domestic product minus indirect taxes
questions.explanation: GNP includes income earned by nationals (including net factor income from abroad). NNP is obtained after subtracting depreciation from GNP. So, NNP = GNP − Depreciation.

questions.question: Consider the following statements regarding Article 166 of the Indian Constitution: I. It deals with the conduct of government business in a state and specifies that all executive actions of the state government must be taken in the name of the Governor. II. It mandates that the Chief Minister shall make rules for the more convenient transaction of business of the state government and for the allocation of said business among the ministers. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Only statement I is correct. Statement I is correct: Article 166(1) explicitly states this. Statement II is incorrect: Article 166(3) mandates that the Governor shall make these rules. While the Governor acts on the advice of the Chief Minister and the Council of Ministers, the constitutional authority to make the rules is vested in the Governor.

questions.question: Consider the following statements regarding the integration of Princely States: i. The Instrument of Accession was the legal document agreed upon by most Princely States to join the Indian Union. ii. The state of Junagadh acceded to India through a plebiscite after its Nawab fled to Pakistan. iii. Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel was the first Governor-General of India who oversaw the integration process. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Statements i and ii are correct, while Statement iii is incorrect. Statement i is correct: The Instrument of Accession was the legal document created in 1947 that allowed the rulers of the princely states to join either India or Pakistan. Statement ii is correct: The Nawab of Junagadh acceded to Pakistan against the wishes of his predominantly Hindu population. After Indian intervention and the flight of the Nawab, a plebiscite was held in February 1948, which resulted in an overwhelming vote in favor of joining India. Statement iii is incorrect: Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel was the first Deputy Prime Minister and Home Minister of India who brilliantly oversaw the integration of the princely states. The first Indian Governor-General was C. Rajagopalachari.

questions.question: "Tee, Par, Flagstick" are associated with _______
questions.explanation: "Tee, Par, Flagstick" are associated with golf. Tee a short plastic stick with a cup-shaped top on which a golf ball is put to be hit, or the area where this is used to start the play for each hole. Flagstick is a removable pole with a flag marking the placement of the hole on each green of a golf course.

questions.question: Consider the following statements about Gini coefficient: I. It is a graphical representation of the distribution of income or of wealth. II. Coefficient of '1' represents perfect inequality. Which of the above statements is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Statement I is incorrect. The Lorenz curve is a graphical representation of the distribution of income or of wealth. It was developed by Max O. Lorenz in 1905 for representing inequality of the wealth distribution. Statement II is correct. The Gini coefficient is a single number that demonstrates a degree of inequality in a distribution of income/wealth. It is used to estimate how far a country's wealth or income distribution deviates from a totally equal distribution. 0 represents perfect equality and 1 represents perfect inequality. Source: NCERT.

questions.question: Kamal bought two items at same price and sold them together at Rs.11440. He sold one item at 10% loss and the other at 30% profit. Calculate the total profit (in rupees) earned by him.
questions.explanation: Let CP of each item = Rs. a Then, SP of one item = (100 - 10)% of a = 0.9a SP of second item = (100 + 30)% of a = 1.3a Now, total SP = 11440 0.9a + 1.3a = 11440 a = 5200 Then, total CP = 2 * 5200 = 10400 Therefore, total profit earned = total SP - total CP = 11440 - 10400 = Rs.1040 Express Method 10% loss = 9/10 = SP/CP 30% profit = 13/10 = SP/CP The ratio of total SP to total CP = 22/20 = 11/10 Given, 11 units = Rs.11440 1 unit = Rs.1040 Total profit earned = Rs.1040

questions.question: Direction: The following table shows the number of phone covers manufactured by a company of four different colors in 5 days.Day/ColorRedBlackBlueGold Day19201080450250 Day2840920350400 Day31020640720800 Day412401080900640 Day5156012401020720 On which day is the total number of phone cover manufactured is minimum?
questions.explanation: On day1, total number of phone cover manufactured = 920 + 1080 + 450 + 250 = 2700 On day2, total number of phone cover manufactured = 840 + 920 + 350 + 400 = 2510 On day3, total number of phone cover manufactured = 1020 + 640 + 720 + 800 = 3180 On day4, total number of phone cover manufactured = 1240 + 1080 + 900 + 640 = 3860 On day2, total number of phone cover manufactured is minimum.

questions.question: Simple interest received on a certain amount for 4 years at 12% rate of interest will be equal to the simple interest received on double of the same amount at what rate of interest for 6 years:
questions.explanation: Let the rate of interest be r% per annum. According to the question, we have ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p20632/image020.png) ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p20632/image021.png) r = 4%

questions.question: Simplify: 625 of 20% + 200 x 40 ÷ 80 - 200
Two containers contain a mixture of milk and water, and the quantity of milk is 64% in the 1st container and 40% in the 2nd container. In what ratio the mixture must be mixed to obtain 46% water?
questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p25162/image004.png) Hence, the required ratio is 7: 5.

questions.question: Simplify the expression: tan(45° + θ) − tan(45° − θ).
questions.explanation: • tan(45° + θ) = (1 + tan θ) / (1 − tan θ). • tan(45° − θ) = (1 − tan θ) / (1 + tan θ). • Subtracting gives: [(1 + tan θ)/(1 − tan θ)] − [(1 − tan θ)/(1 + tan θ)] = [ (1 + tan θ)² − (1 − tan θ)² ] / [ (1 − tan θ)(1 + tan θ) ] = [ (1 + 2tan θ + tan²θ) − (1 − 2tan θ + tan²θ) ] / (1 − tan²θ) = (4 tan θ) / (1 − tan²θ).

questions.question: A thief spots a police car 500 meters away and immediately speeds off on his bike. The police car starts chasing him and eventually catches up. If the thief covers a distance of 2.5 km before being caught, and the police car's speed is 90 km/h, what is the speed of the thief's bike?
questions.explanation: Time taken by thief to cover 2.5 km = Time taken by police to cover (2.5 + 0.5 =) 3 km (2.5)/Speed of thief = 3/90 Speed of thief = 30 x 2.5 = 75 km/h

questions.question: The ratio between the area of the circular base of a cone and its curved surface area is 3: 7. If the slant height of the cone is 14 cm, then find its height?
questions.explanation: Given, πr2/πrl = 3/7 r/l = 3/7 r/14 = 3/7 r = 6 cm The height of the cone = h = √(l2 - r2) = √(196 - 36) = √160 = 4√10 cm

questions.question: In ΔPQR, if PS and QS bisect ∠P and ∠Q respectively, and ∠PSQ = 138°, then find the measure of ∠PRQ.
questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/other/er250129imgchlt001.png) Given PS and QS bisect ∠P and ∠Q. Therefore, S is the incentre of ΔPQR. Now, ∠PSQ = 90 + (∠PRQ/2) 138 = 90 + (∠PRQ/2) ∠PRQ = 48 * 2 = 96°

questions.question: In a right triangle ABC, ∠B = 90° and ∠A = 30°. The side AC is the hypotenuse and measures 20 cm. Find the area of triangle ABC.
questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p27491/image004.png) · Since ∠A = 30° and AC is the hypotenuse (20 cm), the side opposite ∠A is BC. · BC = AC X sin(30°) = 20 X (1/2) = 10 cm. · AB = AC X cos(30°) = 20 X (√3/2) = 10√3 cm. · Area of the right triangle = ½ X AB X BC = ½ X (10√3) X (10) = 50√3 cm². Answer: 50√3 cm²

questions.question: If (x + y + z) = 9 and (x2 + y2 + z2) = 29, then what is the value of (x3 + y3+ z3 - 3xyz)?

A and B can complete a job in 16 days and 24 days respectively. They begin together, but A leaves after t days. B completes the remaining work in 10 days. The total payment is ₹6400 apportioned to A and B in ratio of work done. What is A's share?
questions.explanation: Rate of A = 1/16, Rate of B = 1/24 per day Work done in t days = t(1/16+1/24) = t X 5/48 Work done by B = 10 X 1/24 = 10/24 = 5/12 Total work = t X 5/48 + 5/12 = 1 => multiply 48: 5t + 20 = 48 => 5t = 28 => t = 5.6 days A's work = 5.6 X 1/16 = 0.35 of job A's share = 0.35 X 6400 = ₹2240 Express Method Let total work be LCM of 16 and 24 = 48 units Efficiency of A = 48/16 = 3 units per day Efficiency of B = 48/24 = 2 units per day According to the questions, (3 + 2)t + 10 x 2 = 48 t = 5.6 Part of the work done by A = 3 x 5.6 = 16.8 units Total amount = Rs.6400 Share of A = 16.8 x 6400/48 = Rs. 2240

questions.question: If 4x = 8y = 642z, then the value of 4xz_________y2 is:
questions.explanation: 4x = 8y = 642z 22x = 23y = 212z Since the base is same, 2x = 3y = 12z x/6 = y/4 = z = k (where k is any constant) therefore, x = 6k, y = 4k and z = k Now, in the question expression, 4xz/y2 = (4*6k*k)/(4k*4k) = 24/16 = 3/2

questions.question: The number of books sold by Anil in week1 and in week2 is 2400 and 2000 respectively. While Amit sold 2700 books in week1 and 2400 books in week2. If X and Y are the percentage decrease in the number of books sold by Anil and Amit respectively from week1 to week2, then what will be the ratio of X and Y?
questions.explanation: Percentage decrease in the number of books sold by Anil from week1 to week2 = (2400 - 2000) * 100/2400 = 400/24 % Percentage decrease in the number of books sold by Amit from week1 to week2 = (2700 - 2400) * 100/2700 = 300/27 % Required ratio = (400/24): (300/27) = 3: 2

questions.question: Rs. 2470 is divided among A, B and C in the ratio of 1/5 : 1/4 : 1/2. Find the amount of money received by B.
questions.explanation: Ratio in which amount would be divided 1/5 : 1/4 : 1/2 => 4 : 5 : 10 Total amount = Rs.2470 The amount received by B = 2470 x 5/19 = Rs.650

questions.question: What is the unit digit of 17 + 37 + 57?
questions.explanation: The unit digit of 17 + 37 + 57 will be the unit digit of the sum of unit digits of individual terms. We know the cyclicity of the numbers:NumberCyclicity 1,5,61 4,92 2,3,7,84 So, unit digit of 37 = unit digit of 33 = 7 Unit digit of 57 = 5 Sum = 1 + 7 + 5 = 13 Required unit digit = 3

questions.question: Two persons A and B invested Rs. 12000 and Rs. 15000 respectively. If A invested for 6 months and B invested for 9 months then find the ratio in which the profit should be divided between them.
questions.explanation: Total investment of A = Rs. 12000 * 6 Total investment of B = Rs. 15000 * 9 Ratio in which the profit should be divided = (12000 * 6) : (15000 * 9) = 8 : 15

questions.question: The Parks Unit in Pant Nagar designs a square lawn of side 64 m. A uniform walkway of width 'x' m is laid all around inside the boundary. If the area of the walkway is 960 m2, what is x?
questions.explanation: Square lawn side = 64 m Walkway width = x m Area of walkway = 960 m Total area = 64 = 4096 m Inner square side = (64 - 2x) m Inner area = (64 - 2x) m Area of walkway = Total area - Inner area 4096 - (64 - 2x) = 960 (64 - 2x) = 4096 - 960 = 3136 64 - 2x = √3136 = 56 2x = 64 - 56 = 8 x = 4

questions.question: If (x - 1) and (x - 2) are factors of the polynomial x3 - px + q (with integer coefficients), find the correct pair (p, q).
questions.explanation: Put x = 1 in x3 - px + q we get, 1-p+q = 0 , p-q = 1..(1) Put x = 2 in x3 - px + q 8-2p+q= 0 2p-q= 8..(2) Subtracting (1) and (2) we get, p = 7 and q= 6 Express method: Roots: x=1 and x=2, let third root be r Sum of roots = - Coefficient of x2 / coefficient of x3 1 + 2 + r = 0 r = -3 Product of roots = - constant term / coefficient of x3 1 x 2 x (-3) = -q -6 = -q q = 6 Sum of pairwise products = Coefficient of x / coefficient of x3 (1x2 + 1x(-3) + (2x(-3)) = -p 2 - 3 - 6 = -p -7 =- p p = 7 we get, p=7, q=6

questions.question: The length of the segment of the line 20x + 21y = 420 intercepted between the axes is:
If X = 8(2T - 6) and Y = (5T/2) + 22, then for what value of T, 2X = Y.
The average marks of 80 students are 75. If the marks of one student are mistakenly written as 90 instead of 82, then find the exact average marks of students.
questions.explanation: Total marks of 80 students = 75 * 80 = 6000 Correct total marks = 6000 - 90 + 82 = 5992 Correct average of students = 5992/80 = 74.9 Express Method Decrease in marks = 90 - 82 = 8 So, the average should be decreased by 8/80 = 0.1 Exact average marks = 75 - 0.1 = 74.9

questions.question: If cot A = 3_________2 , then what is the value of 15 sin2A + 3 cos2A_________5 sin2A - 2 cos2A

Consider the given figure where O is the centre of the circle. If ∠ QPR = 60° and ∠ POR = 100° then find the measure of ∠ QOR ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/other/er230308st2np-002.jpg)
questions.explanation: In Δ POR, OP = OR So, ∠ OPR = ∠ ORP = X Since ∠ POR = 100° So, ∠ OPR + ∠ ORP + 100 = 180 2X = 80 X = 40° So, ∠ OPQ = 60 - 40 = 20° In Δ OPQ, OP = OQ So, ∠ OPQ = ∠ OQP = 20° Therefore, ∠ POQ = 180 - (20 + 20) = 140° Now, ∠ QOP = 360 - (100 + 140) = 360 - 240 = 120°

questions.question: The ratio of the volume of a cone to the volume of a cylinder is 16: 15. If the radius of the cone and the cylinder is 8 cm and 5 cm respectively and the height of the cone is 3 cm more than the height of the cylinder, then find the height of the cylinder.
questions.explanation: Let the height of the cylinder be 'H' The volume of the cone/ The volume of a cylinder = 16/15 (1/3 * π * r2 * h)/ (π * R2 * H) = 16/ 15 [8 * 8 * (H + 3)]/ [3 * 5 * 5 * H] = 16/15 [8 * (H + 3)]/ 5H = 2/1 8H + 24 = 10H 2H = 24 H = 12 Hence, the height of the cylinder is 12 cm.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Hoard
questions.explanation: Hoard: to collect and often hide away a supply of. Squander: waste (something, especially money or time) in a reckless and foolish manner. As per the meanings, both words are opposite to each other. Hence, option (a) is the answer. Garner: to acquire by effort. Amass: gather together or accumulate (a large amount or number of material or things) over a period of time. Scrimp: to make it too small, short, or scanty.

questions.question: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in reported speech. She said, “I have passed the test with full marks.”
options: She said, she has passed the test with full marks.
She said that she had passed the test with full marks.
She said she passed the test with full marks.
She said that she have passed the test with full marks.
questions.explanation: The given sentence is in direct speech. In the indirect speech, the reporting verb (said) will be joined to the reported sentence (I have passed the test with full marks) by the conjunction 'that'. Also, since the reporting verb is in past tense, the tense in the reported speech will be changed. The direct speech is in present perfect tense (have passed). In indirect speech, this should be converted to past perfect tense (had passed). Hence, option B is the correct answer.

questions.question: Describe how you will say Those paintings were admired by the visitors In active voice.
questions.explanation: The subject of the Passive Voice is changed into the object of the Active Voice. Also, the object of the Passive Voice is changed into the subject of the Active Voice. The removal of the "to be" verb [were] and the preposition 'by' is necessary while changing the voice of the sentence from Passive to Active. The Passive voice in this question is in Past Indefinite. Thus, the Active voice will also be in Past Indefinite. In Passive voice, the format is: Object of the Active voice (Subjective case) + was/were + V3 + by + Subject of the Active voice (Objective case). Thus, the format of Active voice is: Subject + V2 + Object. For example: The Champions League was won by Real Madrid. (Passive Voice) Real Madrid won the Champions League. (Active Voice) The options except (d) don't follow the correct grammatical format of the Active Voice. Therefore, the correct answer is: The visitors admired those paintings. Hence, option (d) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Sentences of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful and coherent paragraph. A. These biases affect the quality of many other interactions in clinics, and it is not only limited to black patients only. B. Doctors spend less time and build less emotional rapport with obese patients as well as transgender people face overt prejudice and discrimination. C. Black patients are given fewer therapeutic procedures, even though it depends upon insurance, illness severity and type of hospital. D. In case of heart attacks too, black people are less likely to receive guideline-based care; in intensive care units for heart failure, they are less likely to see a cardiologist, which is linked to survival.
questions.explanation: ·Identifying the first sentence in the paragraph is a basic step to solve the parajumble. Among the given sentences, C can be easily identified as the introductory sentence as it throws light on the manner in which black patients are treated in the hospitals. ·D is in continuation of C as it tells us how black patients are treated in case of heart failures too. ·Thereafter, A carries forward the paragraph as it throws light on the fact that discrimination in the medical field is not only faced by black patients but by others as well. ·B is the concluding sentence as it tells us how trans and obese people also face discrimination in the medical field. ·Therefore, the correct answer is:(d) CDAB.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Novice
questions.explanation: Beginner- a person just starting to learn a skill or take part in an activity. Veteran- a person who has had long experience in a particular field. Pacifier- a person or thing that pacifies someone or something. Supervisee- one who is supervised, and works under a supervisor. The meaning of the word 'novice' is 'a person new to and inexperienced in a job or situation'. Among the options, the word that is opposite in meaning to the given word is option (b), 'veteran'. Hence, the correct answer is option (b).

questions.question: Select the sentence that has no spelling error.
options: It is the most funddamental concept of programming.
Fundamantal principles of the theorem are violated.
The fundamental idea of the project is very good but needs to be developed further.
questions.explanation: Among the given sentences, sentence (c) alone has no spelling errors. The word 'fundamental' is wrongly spelt in options a), b), and d), and correctly spelt in option c). 'Fundamental' means 'forming a necessary base or core; of central importance'. Hence, the correct answer is option (c).

questions.question: Select the correct sentence in passive voice from the following options.
options: The horror movie had been directed by Steven.
Steven had directed the horror movie.
Steven would have been directing the horror movie.
questions.explanation: In passive voice, the subject of the sentence receives the action rather than performing it. Option (a) is in passive voice because the subject (the horror movie) is receiving the action (being directed) by Steven. Hence, (a) is the correct answer. The rest of the options are in the active voice.

questions.question: Select the appropriate idiom for the given blank. I decided I had to ­­­­______________ and take a couple of math classes even though I knew they were hard.
questions.explanation: The sentence talks about a difficult situation where the speaker knows that the math classes are going to be tough. But he/she will still have to attend them. "Bite the bullet" means to decide to do something difficult or unpleasant that one has been putting off or hesitating over; "run like the wind" means move or run very quickly; "have your head in clouds" means to not know the facts of a situation; "go down in flames" means to fail spectacularly or to a great degree. According to these meanings and the given sentence, only option a) is appropriate for the blank. It implies that I decided to undertake the math classes even though they were hard, and I was unwilling to take them. Hence, a) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. Call into question
questions.explanation: Call into question- to raise a question or doubt about something. Option C is the correct answer.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word. Punctilious
questions.explanation: 'Punctilious' means showing great attention to detail or correct behaviour. 'Perfectionist' means a person who wants everything to be perfect and demands the highest standards possible. Hence, option (d) is the answer.

questions.question: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the underlined group of words. After years of writing, he finally decided to compile his best works into a collection of poems.
questions.explanation: 'Anthology' is the appropriate one-word substitute for the underlined group of words. The term 'anthology' describes a collection of literary works, such as poems, short stories, or plays, typically chosen for their merit or relevance. 'Claque' refers to a group of people hired to applaud at a performance. 'Bale' refers to a large bundle of goods, such as hay or cotton, or a large quantity of something. 'Caravan' refers to a group of travellers, typically in a convoy of vehicles, or a vehicle equipped for living or travelling. Hence, (c) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word.
questions.explanation: Option (c) is incorrectly spelt. The correct word is 'substantial'. Hence, option (c) is the answer. Contemptuous - manifesting, feeling, or expressing deep hatred or disapproval. Especially - used to single out one person or thing over all others. Substantial - of considerable importance, size, or worth. Tannery - a place where animal hides are tanned; the workshop of a tanner.

questions.question: From among the words given in bold, select the incorrectly spelt word in the following sentence. The comerrcial space industry is expanding, with private companies contributing to space exploration and satellite deployment.
questions.explanation: Among the given options, the word 'comerrcial' is incorrectly spelt. Commercial: Relating to or engaged in commerce, business, or trade. Hence, option (c) is the correct answer. Exploration: The systematic investigation of a subject or theme. Deployment: The movement or positioning of resources for a specific purpose or task.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. The tears streamed __________ while she was writing the letter.
questions.explanation: Option C is the right answer, as the word 'incessantly' means continuously and fits most appropriately in the context of the sentence. Option A is eliminated, as the word 'importantly' does not fit in the context of the sentence. Option B is eliminated as the word 'vivaciously' means 'in a way that is attractively energetic and enthusiastic' and it does not fit in contextually. Option D is eliminated as the word 'instantly' would not bring out the meaning of the sentence.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill blank (1).
questions.explanation: After a cursory reading, we can figure that the author/speaker in the passage talks about a book of her/his in which s/he has shared the outcome of her/his study of climate change. 'Learning' (knowledge acquired through study, experience) is the outcome of studying something and looking at the sentence, we see that in the blank (1), we need a verb in its past participle form (so that 'have + past participle' forms a present perfect tense construct; to indicate that the process of 'learning' has already happened before the speaker/author started writing the book). Combining these ideas, we see that 'learned' should be the best word to replace blank (1). ('Booked' is the past participle of 'book' which means 'reserve (a seat etc.); or make an official note of the personal details of (a person who has broken a law or rule)'; doesn't fit contextually here) The answer is option (c).

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill blank (2).
questions.explanation: Writing a book to destroy (end the existence of something) or bully (seek to harm, or intimidate someone vulnerable) does not make sense. 'Alternative' (a noun meaning 'one of two or more available possibilities'; or an adjective meaning '(of one or more things) available as another possibility') is grammatically incorrect because we need a verb (in its base form) to make the 'to-infinitive' structure, to indicate the action. 'Explain' is the most appropriate alternative to replace the blank (2). The answer is option (d).

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill blank (3).
questions.explanation: Here, we need a verb (in its base form) to make the 'to-infinitive' structure; thus, indicating the action. 'Constructed' (past participle); 'scientific' (adjective); and 'year' (noun) are grammatically unfit to replace the blank. 'Build' (strengthen) is the only good choice to replace the blank (3). The answer is option (c).

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill blank (4).
questions.explanation: The use of 'some' in the sentence with blank (4) indicates that the required word should be a plural noun that can be qualified by the adjective 'concrete' (tangible; definite). Options (a) and (b) are singular nouns and hence are grammatically unfit here. Now the usage 'make it happen' requires one to make an effort and hence the word 'bricks' (rectangular blocks typically made of fired or sun-dried clay, used in building) doesn't make sense in the context. 'Steps' (measures; efforts) is the most appropriate choice here as 'concrete steps' (real/tangible measures) would result in 'making it happen'. The answer is option (d).

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill blank (5).
questions.explanation: The required word should be a noun (because the determiner 'the' is used). 'Alarming' (worrying; disturbing) is an adjective and hence grammatically unfit. 'Festival' (a day or period of celebration) and 'house' are contextual misfits as the author does not touch upon these phenomena. 'Book' is the central theme of this passage and hence should be the most appropriate word to replace blank (5). The answer is option (a).

questions.question: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the underlined group of words. She spent several years living in a residence for nuns, where she found both spiritual fulfilment and a strong sense of community.
questions.explanation: 'Convent' is the appropriate one-word substitute for the underlined group of words. The term 'convent' describes a community of people, especially nuns, living together in a building, or buildings. 'Arsenal' refers to a collection of weapons and military equipment or a place where they are stored. 'Aviary' refers to a large enclosure for keeping birds in. 'Arena' refers to a place or situation in which people compete or fight. Hence, (c) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate idiom that can substitute the underlined words in the given sentence. The manager gave the team a rap over the hands for missing the deadline, but no serious action was taken.
questions.explanation: "A rap over the knuckles": A mild reprimand or scolding, often for a minor mistake or wrongdoing. Hence, option (b) is correct.

questions.question: Select the option with the correct use of present tense.
options: She studies every evening and practises piano for an hour before going to bed.
She study every evening and practise piano for an hour before going to bed.
She studies every evening and practise piano for an hour before going to bed.
She is studied every evening and practised piano for an hour before going to bed.
questions.explanation: Option a) correctly uses the present simple tense ("studies" and "practices") to describe regular, habitual actions that occur in the present. Hence, option (a) is correct. Option (b): The plural verbs "study" and "practise" cannot be used for the singular subject "she". They need to be replaced with singular verbs "studies" and "practises". Option (c): Here also, the plural verb "practise" is incorrect for the singular subject "she". It should be the singular verb "practises". Option (d): It uses the wrong tense ("is studied" is passive and refers to someone else doing the action to her), which changes the meaning and makes it incorrect. It should be 'she studied'. Note: Active and Passive Voice: When the person doing the action is the subject, the sentence is said to be in active form/voice. When the subject is what the action is directed at, then the sentence is said to be in passive form/voice. For example: Active Voice: Rita and her friends performed a skit. Here 'Rita and her friends' (subject) perform the action. Passive Voice: A skit was performed by Rita and her friends. Here 'skit' is the subject (at which the action is directed).

questions.question: Select the appropriate option to replace the underlined word with adverb of time. We have a meeting scheduled for usually.
questions.explanation: 'Regular' and 'once' are not adverbs of time. So, they are eliminated. The sentence is not in the past tense. So, the use of 'yesterday' is inappropriate in the given sentence. Among the given options, 'tomorrow' is the correct choice. Hence, option (a) is the correct answer. The correct sentence is: We have a meeting scheduled for tomorrow. Note: Adverb of Time: It tells us when an action is done or occurs. Examples: after, early, now, soon, then.

questions.question: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in positive degree of comparison. Rohan is faster than Shubam in terms of running.
options: Shubam is slower at running than Rohan.
Shubam is not as fast a runner as Rohan.
Running-wise, Rohan is faster than Shubam.
Rohan is the fastest runner among all.
questions.explanation: To express the given sentence in a positive degree of comparison, we need to state the comparison in a way that focuses on Rohan's speed in running without explicitly mentioning Shubam as slower. In option (b), we emphasise Rohan's speed without directly comparing it to Shubam's. The phrase "not as fast as" implies that Rohan is the faster runner without explicitly stating that Shubam is slower. This maintains a positive tone in the competition. Hence, (b) is the correct answer. Options (a) and (c) are in comparative degree of comparison. Option (d) is in the superlative degree of comparison. Note: · The Positive Degree is used to denote the mere existence of some quality of what we speak about. It is used when no comparison is made. · The Comparative degree denotes a higher degree of quality than the positive and is used when two things are compared. · The Superlative degree indicates the highest degree of quality and is used when more than two things (or sets of things) are compared.

questions.question: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error. People with chronic insomnia / experience difficulties falling / or staying asleep at least / three night per week.
questions.explanation: Option (c) has an error. In option (c), the numerical value 'three' indicates the number of the noun 'night' that follows it and the noun 'night' being singular is incorrect here because 'three' indicates more than one, but the singular noun 'night' incorrectly follows the plural numerical quantifier. The singular noun must be replaced by the plural form of the noun, 'nights'. The correct sentence is: People with chronic insomnia experience difficulties falling or staying asleep at least three nights per week. Hence, the correct answer is option (c). Note: Quantifier: A word or phrase that is used before a noun to show the amount of it that is being considered: "Some", "many", "a lot of", and "a few" are examples of quantifiers.
